category_title: Low-proficiency international students with high linguistic anxiety and reliance on external support
minimal_context: The student is writing a 100-word comment on a peer's blog post after giving a presentation.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please write a 100-word comment on your classmate's blog post about their presentation.

STUDENT: I think their presentation was really good in the class. I did the presentation after them and I felt a little tense, because they did it so good.

TUTOR: What else can you write in your comment?

STUDENT: In overall their English are very good.

TUTOR: You need to write a bit more to reach the word count.

STUDENT: I will just write: I hope we can be friends and you can tell me how you study English well.

options.A: Encourage the student to change their self-label from being a 'bad student' to a normal student who 'happens to have difficulty with a few specific behaviors.'
options.B: Remain a silent observer during the activity and provide a full grade as long as the student makes a timely blog comment.
options.C: Do activities related to the blog post with the student, using songs, games (puzzles, Q & A, TPR), drawing, story chains, and role play.
options.D: Guide the process of self-management by helping the student identify situations that trigger anger responses during the activity and generate ideas about alternative behaviors.
